name: threads
context: Manage Assistants API threads — create threads, add messages, run assistants, and retrieve responses
info: Threads are the conversation containers in the Assistants API. A thread holds an ordered sequence of messages and manages context window state automatically. The workflow is: create a thread, add user messages, create a run to execute an assistant against the thread, poll or stream for completion, then read the assistant's response messages. Threads persist server-side until explicitly deleted, enabling multi-session conversations. Each thread is independent — one assistant can serve many threads simultaneously. Thread messages support text, images, and file attachments. See [[openai/assistants/threads/create]] for thread creation and [[openai/assistants/threads/run]] for executing runs.
triggers: create a thread for an assistant

sections.title: Creating threads
sections.body: Call `client.beta.threads.create()` with optional initial messages and metadata. Threads are lightweight — create one per conversation. The returned thread object contains an `id` used for all subsequent operations. Threads can be created with messages pre-loaded for convenience. See [[openai/assistants/threads/create]] for details.

sections.title: Thread messages
sections.body: Add messages with `client.beta.threads.messages.create(threadId, { role, content })`. The `role` is either `user` or `assistant`. Messages support text content, image URLs, and file attachments (for code interpreter or file search). List messages with `.list()`, retrieve with `.retrieve()`. Messages are ordered by creation time.

sections.title: Running assistants on threads
sections.body: Create a run with `client.beta.threads.runs.create(threadId, { assistant_id })` to execute an assistant against the thread's messages. Poll with `.retrieve()` until `status` is `completed`, `failed`, or `requires_action`. Use `.createAndPoll()` for automatic polling or `.stream()` for real-time output. See [[openai/assistants/threads/run]] for the full run lifecycle.

sections.title: Context window management
sections.body: Threads automatically handle context window limits. When messages exceed the model's context, older messages are truncated. Control truncation with `truncation_strategy` on runs: `auto` (default) or `last_messages` with a count. Use `max_prompt_tokens` to cap total input tokens. Long conversations should use thread metadata to track state outside the context window.

links.context: Create threads and add initial messages

related.note: For stateless single-turn interactions without persistent threads

scripts.name: Thread Conversation
scripts.description: Create a thread, add a message, run an assistant, and retrieve the response

USER_MESSAGE.description: User message to add to the thread

THREAD_ID.description: Existing thread ID to continue (creates new if omitted)

assistantId.description: ID of the assistant to run on the thread

threadId.description: Existing thread ID (omit to create a new thread)

messages.description: Messages to add to the thread

metadata.description: Key-value metadata to attach to the thread (max 16 pairs, keys max 64 chars, values max 512 chars)

depends.reason: Threads are part of the Assistants API

author: dojo-community
